questions.question: A cuboid has length 10 cm, breadth 8 cm, height 6 cm. Find its total surface area and volume.
A cube has surface area 216 cm². Find its side and volume.
questions.answer: 6a² = 216 ⇒ a² = 36 ⇒ a = 6 cm; Volume = a³ = 6³ = 216 cm³.

questions.question: The radius of a sphere is 7 cm. Find its surface area and volume.
Find the total surface area and volume of a hemisphere with radius 10 cm.
A cylinder has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm. Find its curved surface area, total surface area and volume.
A cone has radius 6 cm and height 8 cm. Find its slant height, curved surface area, total surface area and volume.
A solid sphere of radius 14 cm is melted to form smaller spheres each of radius 7 cm. Find the number of smaller spheres formed.
questions.answer: Volume of large sphere = (4/3)π × 14³ = 1144π; Volume of one small sphere = (4/3)π × 7³ = 143.67π; Number of spheres = 1144π / 143.67π ≈ 8.

questions.question: Water in a hemispherical bowl of radius 21 cm is poured into a cylindrical vessel of radius 14 cm. Find the height of water in the cylinder.
questions.answer: Volume of hemisphere = (2/3)πr³ = (2/3) × π × 9261 = 6174π; Volume of cylinder = πr²h ⇒ 6174π = π × 196 × h ⇒ h = 31.5 cm.

questions.question: The diameter of a sphere is 14 cm. Find the diameter of a cylinder with same volume and radius 7 cm. Find height of cylinder.
questions.answer: Volume of sphere = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π × 7³ = 1436.8 cm³; Cylinder volume = πr²h ⇒ 1436.8 = π × 49 × h ⇒ h ≈ 9.3 cm.

questions.question: A solid cone of height 24 cm and base radius 7 cm is melted to form smaller cones each of radius 7 cm and height 3 cm. Find the number of smaller cones formed.
questions.answer: Volume of large cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π × 49 × 24 = 392π; Volume of one small cone = (1/3)π × 49 × 3 = 49π; Number of cones = 392π / 49π = 8.

questions.question: Find the curved surface area of a frustum of cone with radii 7 cm and 14 cm and slant height 10 cm.
A sphere is cut into two hemispheres. Find the surface area of each hemisphere if radius = 10 cm.
questions.answer: CSA of hemisphere = 2πr² = 2 × π × 100 = 200π; TSA = 3πr² = 300π ≈ 942 cm².

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 14 cm is melted to form small spheres each of radius 7 cm. Find the total surface area of all small spheres.
questions.answer: Number of spheres = 8; surface area of one small sphere = 4πr² = 4 × π × 49 = 196π; Total surface area = 8 × 196π = 1568π ≈ 4922 cm².

questions.question: Water is poured from a cylindrical vessel of radius 7 cm and height 24 cm into hemispherical bowls of radius 7 cm. Find the number of bowls filled.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π × 49 × 24 = 1176π; Volume of one hemisphere = (2/3)πr³ = (2/3) × π × 343 ≈ 228.67π; Number of bowls = 1176 / 228.67 ≈ 5.

questions.question: The radius of a sphere increases by 10%. Find the percentage increase in its volume.
questions.answer: Volume ∝ r³ ⇒ increase = (1.1³ - 1) × 100% = 33.1%.

questions.question: The height of a cone is doubled keeping the base radius same. Find the percentage increase in volume.
questions.answer: Volume ∝ h; new volume = 2 × old volume ⇒ increase = 100%.

questions.question: The radius of a cylinder is halved keeping height same. Find the percentage decrease in volume.
questions.answer: Volume ∝ r²; new volume = (1/4) × old volume ⇒ decrease = 75%.

questions.question: A sphere and a cylinder have same volume. The radius of sphere = 7 cm, radius of cylinder = 7 cm. Find height of cylinder.
questions.answer: V(sphere) = V(cylinder); (4/3)π × 343 = π × 49 × h ⇒ h = 28/3 ≈ 9.33 cm.

questions.question: A cone has base radius 3 cm and height 4 cm. A sphere of radius 3 cm is placed inside it. Find the volume of empty space in cone.
questions.answer: V(cone) = (1/3)π × 9 × 4 = 12π; V(sphere) = (4/3)π × 27 = 36π/3 = 12π; Empty space = 12π - 12π = 0. Sphere exactly fits.

questions.question: A cylinder of radius 7 cm and height 14 cm is melted to form smaller spheres of radius 2 cm. Find number of spheres formed.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = π × 49 × 14 = 686π; Volume of small sphere = (4/3)π × 8 = (32/3)π; Number of spheres = 686π / (32/3)π = 686 × 3 / 32 ≈ 64.

questions.question: A frustum of cone has radii 7 cm and 14 cm, slant height 10 cm. Find its volume.
A spherical vessel of radius 21 cm is full of water. Find the total surface area and volume of water in liters.
questions.answer: Surface area = 4πr² = 4 × π × 441 = 1764π ≈ 5537 cm²; Volume = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π × 9261 ≈ 38838 cm³ ≈ 38.838 liters.

questions.question: A cylinder of radius 10 cm and height 21 cm is melted into spheres of radius 7 cm. Find number of spheres.
questions.answer: Volume of cylinder = π × 100 × 21 = 2100π; Volume of one sphere = (4/3)π × 343 = 457.33π; Number of spheres ≈ 2100 / 457.33 ≈ 4.59 ⇒ 4 spheres fully.

questions.question: A cylindrical tank of radius 14 m and height 3 m is filled with water. Find the volume of water in liters and surface area of tank.
questions.answer: Volume = πr²h = π × 196 × 3 = 588π ≈ 1847 m³ ≈ 1.847 × 10⁶ liters; Surface area = 2πr(h + r) = 2 × π × 14 × 17 = 476π ≈ 1495 m².

questions.question: The radius of a spherical balloon increases from 14 cm to 21 cm. Find the percentage increase in volume.
